title: salix × brachypurpurea
intro: Salix × brachypurpurea, also known as purple osier willow or purple willow, is a hybrid plant that belongs to the Salix genus and is a cross between Salix purpurea and Salix viminalis. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which are attributed to the presence of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Salix × brachypurpurea include relief from pain, reduction of inflammation, and support for the cardiovascular system. Salix × brachypurpurea is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or salve for topical application, and it is also used in traditional folk medicine for its antiseptic and antispasmodic properties.
